old_claim.note: Reed Mat Architecture provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the Dhra raised-floor granaries use mud, timber, reeds, and storage design rather than the later regional reed-architecture tradition.

source_shape.source_locator: Historical discussion of mudhif and reed architecture as a southern Mesopotamian architectural tradition.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports reed architecture as a regional building tradition, not as a necessary precursor for the earlier Dhra raised-floor granaries.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The directly excavated granary construction has its own mud-wall and suspended-floor evidence, so the later regional architecture node is not causal.

ontology_before: The duplicate granary record carried a broad reed-architecture enabler edge that was only a material association.
ontology_after: The consolidated Dhra granary node no longer depends directly on later reed architecture.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-10-ancient-materials-scope keeps this removed edge absent and locks the corrected early-material chronology.
would_reject_if: The dependent node were narrowed to a later application that truly requires the removed prerequisite.
A stronger source showed the removed prerequisite existed earlier than and was necessary for the dependent node scope.
A future graph split introduced a narrower downstream node where this removed edge would be correctly scoped.
short_reason: Dhra raised-floor granaries do not depend on the later scoped reed-architecture tradition.
